sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content:
The test dashboard previously relied on two disjoint data sources: the dFlow DB for T2/T3 tests (via `/api/tests`) and the cortex DB for T1 tests (via `/api/t1_tests`). Tier classification depended on `covers_count` from dFlow's `coverage_links` table, but AST call graph `validates` edges replaced `covers=` as the source of test-to-code linking.

The dashboard has been migrated to a cortex-native architecture: a unified `/api/tests` endpoint queries cortex DB for test node discovery and dFlow DB (optionally) for tier classification. Test results come from cortex's `test_results` table. A `POST /api/test/run` endpoint triggers test runs from the browser. The `cortex.toml` `[cortex.scan] test_paths` option prevents test functions from appearing in the Workflows tab.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-TD-01 — See all tests with pass/fail status**
As a developer, I want to open the test dashboard and immediately see every test in my project with its latest pass/fail/skip status so that I can tell at a glance whether my changes broke anything.

**US-TD-02 — Group tests by module**
As a developer, I want to group tests by module so that I can focus on the test files relevant to the code I'm changing.

**US-TD-03 — Group tests by tier**
As a developer, I want to see tests grouped by annotation tier (T1/T2/T3) so that I can understand how well-documented my test suite is.

**US-TD-04 — See which production functions a test validates**
As a developer, I want to click a test and see the list of production functions it directly calls (from cortex `validates` edges) so that I understand what code this test is actually exercising.

**US-TD-05 — Run tests from the dashboard**
As a developer, I want to click a "Run" button on a test so that I can trigger a test run and see updated results without switching to the CLI.

**US-TD-06 — Run all tests in a group**
As a developer, I want a "Run all" button on any group header — whether tests are grouped by module, tier, or any other grouping — so that I can run every test in that group and see the results update in place, regardless of how I've chosen to organize the list.

**US-TD-07 — Smart grouping availability**
As a developer, I want the dashboard to only offer grouping options that are meaningful for my project — e.g. if no tests have `@workflow` annotations, tier grouping should be hidden or disabled with a hint like "all tests are T1 — add @workflow to enable tier grouping" — so that I'm not presented with useless grouping options.

**US-TD-08 — Group by pass/fail status**
As a developer, I want to group tests by their latest result (pass/fail/skip/no result) so that I can quickly focus on failing tests, even when I haven't annotated my tests with tiers.

**US-TD-09 — Module groups collapsed by default**
As a developer, I want module groups to be collapsed by default when grouping by module, showing only the module name and a pass/fail summary count (e.g. `test_scanner.py — 12 passed, 1 failed`), so that I can scan all modules at a glance without scrolling through a wall of individual test rows. I click a group header to expand it.

**US-TD-10 — Search tests by name**
As a developer, I want a live search box that filters the visible test list by function name so that I can jump to a specific test inside a large module without scrolling, independently of which module is selected in the module checklist.

**US-TD-11 — Failures surface immediately on open**
As a developer, I want the dashboard to default to showing only failing tests when failures exist so that I see what's broken the moment I open the tab, without having to manually apply a filter. When all tests pass, the default reverts to showing all tests.

**US-TD-12 — Reliable tier classification**
As a developer, I want tier classification (T1/T2/T3) to be accurate even when two test files contain functions with the same name, so that renaming or duplicating a test never silently changes another test's tier badge.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Three-panel layout (filters / list / detail + Monaco) | Done |
| Tier badges (T1/T2/T3) | Done |
| Tier classification from dFlow DB (decorator + step detection) | Done |
| Test results from cortex `test_results` table | Done |
| Filter toggles (steps, critical, validates, tier) | Done |
| Group by module / tier | Done |
| Group by pass/fail status | Done |
| Smart grouping (hide tier when all T1) | Done |
| Monaco source viewer with line highlighting | Done |
| Fixture linking via cortex `test:fixture` tag | Done |
| Module checklist with text search | Done |
| Cortex-native unified test endpoint (`/api/tests`) | Done |
| Validates edge display in detail panel | Done |
| Run tests from dashboard (single test) | Done |
| Run tests from dashboard (group — any grouping) | Done |
| Run all tests button | Done |
| `cortex.toml` test_paths filtering for Workflows tab | Done |
| Module-level node exclusion (`atomic_process` filter) | Done |
| Correct line-number highlighting (no fallback to line 1) | Done |
| Step display for T2/T3 tests | Not started |
| Module groups collapsed by default with summary counts (US-TD-09) | Not started |
| Name search box within test list (US-TD-10) | Not started |
| Failing-first default on dashboard open (US-TD-11) | Not started |
| Tier classification by (name + file path) join, not name alone (US-TD-12) | Not started |

sections.heading: Tier Classification
sections.content:
Tiers classify tests by annotation level. Classification uses two data sources: cortex DB for test node discovery and the dFlow DB for `@workflow` decorator and step detection.

| Tier | Rule | What it means |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | No `@workflow` decorator in dFlow DB | Plain pytest function. Good docstrings are sufficient. |
| T2 | `@workflow(purpose=...)` present, `step_count == 0` | Annotated entry point — signals intent without requiring a full narrative. |
| T3 | `@workflow(purpose=...)` present, `step_count > 0` | Fully narrated test with `Step()` markers — readable without opening source. |

**Key change:** `covers_count` is no longer a tier signal. Tiers are about annotation level (decorator + steps). Coverage is orthogonal — `validates_count` (from cortex `validates` edges) is a separate display field showing how many production functions this test directly calls.

**Data source:** The viz server queries the dFlow DB directly (`_dflow_connect()`) to find functions with `@workflow` decorators (role `'workflow'`) and their step counts. It matches these by function name against cortex test nodes. The `validates` edge count comes from a cortex edge query.

**Graceful degradation:** When the dFlow DB (`.dflow/workflow.db`) is absent, all tests are classified as T1. The dashboard still works — tier grouping is hidden via the smart grouping logic (US-TD-07).

**Test path filtering:** The `[cortex.scan] test_paths` option in `cortex.toml` defines path prefixes for test directories (e.g. `["tests/"]`). The `/api/workflows` endpoint uses this to exclude `@workflow`-decorated test functions from the Workflows tab, since test functions share the same `@workflow` decorator as production workflows.

sections.heading: Data Architecture
sections.content:
### Implemented Architecture

```
/api/tests     -> cortex DB (nodes tagged 'test', node_type='atomic_process')
                  + cortex DB validates edges for coverage counts
                  + cortex DB test_results for pass/fail status
                  + dFlow DB (optional) for @workflow detection and step counts
/api/t1_tests  -> deprecated, returns empty list
/api/fixtures  -> cortex DB (test:fixture tag) — unchanged
/api/test/run  -> POST: triggers cortex test subprocess, returns results
/api/workflows -> dFlow DB filtered by cortex.toml test_paths
```

**Key design decisions:**
- Test node discovery is cortex-only (nodes tagged `test`, filtered to `atomic_process` to exclude module-level nodes)
- Tier classification queries the dFlow DB separately by function name matching — not via `dflow_meta` on cortex nodes
- When dFlow DB is absent, all tests default to T1 (graceful degradation)
- The `/api/workflows` endpoint reads `[cortex.scan] test_paths` from `cortex.toml` to filter out `@workflow`-decorated functions in test directories

### Response shape per test item
```json
{
  "cortex_id": "proj::tests.test_foo::test_bar",
  "name": "test_bar",
  "module": "tests/test_foo.py",
  "location": "tests/test_foo.py",
  "line_start": 42,
  "line_end": 68,
  "docstring": "...",
  "has_workflow": true,
  "step_count": 3,
  "validates_count": 2,
  "validates": ["proj::pm.method::execute", "proj::pm.cli::main"],
  "tier": "T3",
  "result": "passed",
  "result_timestamp": "2026-03-13T14:22:00"
}
```

sections.heading: Run Tests from Dashboard
sections.content:
### How it works

The dashboard triggers test runs by calling a POST endpoint on the viz server, which delegates to the same runner logic as `cortex test` — detecting the runner, parsing results, and persisting to the `test_results` table.

**Endpoint:** `POST /api/test/run`

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `path` | string (optional) | pytest path prefix, e.g. `tests/test_scanner.py` — runs all tests in that file/directory |
| `node_id` | string (optional) | cortex node ID — runs tests scoped to that node via `validates` edges |

Exactly one of `path` or `node_id` must be provided. Omit both to run all tests.

**Behavior:**
- Server runs pytest as a subprocess using the auto-detected runner
- One run at a time — subsequent requests return 409 Conflict while a run is in progress
- UI disables all run buttons and shows a spinner during execution
- On completion, server returns structured results (pass/fail counts, run_id)
- Dashboard auto-refreshes the test list with updated results

**"Run" on group headers:**
Every group header — whether grouped by module, tier, or pass/fail status — gets a "Run" button. Clicking it calls `POST /api/test/run` with `path` set to the common directory prefix of the group's tests. This scopes the pytest run to that group regardless of how tests are grouped.

**"Run" on individual tests:**
Each test list item has a small run button that scopes the run to that test's module file.

**"Run all" button:**
A prominent "Run all tests" button sits at the top of the filter panel and runs the full test suite.

### Safety

The subprocess concern is contained:
- The viz server constructs the pytest command using the same runner detection as `cortex test`
- The viz server is local-only (localhost, no auth needed)
- One-at-a-time execution prevents resource contention
- No custom pytest args from the UI — only scoping parameters

### Deferred
- Live stdout streaming from pytest into the dashboard
- Parallel test runs
- Cancellation mid-run
- Custom pytest args from the UI

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
**Step display for T2/T3** — Steps come from dFlow `steps` table via `@workflow` + `Step()` markers. If dFlow is absent, step display gracefully degrades (shows 0 steps, test classified as T2 not T3).

**Module groups collapsed by default with summary counts (US-TD-09)** — When grouping by module, groups render collapsed showing only module name + pass/fail count. Click to expand. This is the primary fix for the tests tab scaling problem.

**Name search box within test list (US-TD-10)** — Live text filter on function name, independent of the module checklist. Two-level filtering: module checklist narrows by file, name search narrows within the visible set.

**Failing-first default on open (US-TD-11)** — When the dashboard loads and failing tests exist, default the result filter to "failed". When all tests pass, default to showing all tests.

**Tier classification by (name + file path) join (US-TD-12)** — Tighten `/api/tests` dFlow join from bare function name to `(function_name, file_path)`. Fixes silent misclassification when two test files contain functions with the same name. The dFlow `functions` table has a `module_id` FK to `modules.path` which provides the file path for matching.

**Live stdout streaming** — Stream pytest output into the dashboard during a test run.

**Parallel test runs / cancellation** — One-at-a-time is the current constraint.

**Custom pytest args from UI** — Currently only scoping parameters (`path`, `node_id`) are supported.
